name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value strictly greater than the original input

name: align_zero_nonzero
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_zero_nonzero(b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(0usize, b) != 0usize,
{
}
reason: Aligning zero to any positive alignment must yield zero since 0 mod b == 0

name: aligned_identity_broken
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_identity_broken(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: An already-aligned address must be a fixed point of align_to_usize; otherwise the spec is inconsistent with aligned

name: one_alignment_false
code:
proof fn phi_4_one_alignment_false(addr: nat)
    requires
        true,
    ensures
        !aligned(addr, 1nat),
{
}
reason: Every address is trivially 1-aligned since addr % 1 == 0 for all addr

name: align_result_unaligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_result_unaligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The whole purpose of align_to_usize is to produce an aligned result; if the output is unaligned the spec is wrong
